rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Depositions Of The Person Most Qualified And Custodian Of Records Of Defendant Cahill Construction Co., Inc., Or In The Alternative, Motion For An Order Imposing Terminating Sanctions, Monetary Sanctions, And Evidentiary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, March 13, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel the depositions of the person most qualified and custodian of records of defendant Cahill Construction Co., Inc., or in the alternative, motion to impose sanctions: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Cahill Construction Co., Inc.S Further Verified Discovery Responses And Deeming Plaintiffs Requests For Admission Admitted; And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, March 13, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel defendant Cahill Construction Co., Inc. to provide further responses to plaintiffs¿ special interrogatories nos. 27-28, 55-57, 73-74, 77-98 and requests for production nos. 9, 13-16, 18-21, 24, 34, 36-41, 46, 48, 51, 53, 55, 56-65 is granted. Defendant¿s responses are inadequate. Defendant has 15 days to serve supplemental responses. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel defendant to verify its responses is denied. Defendant, a dissolved corporation, is under no obligation to provide verifications. (Maldonado v. Superior Court (2002) 94 Cal.App.4th 1390.) Since defendant is not required to provide verifications, defendant responded to plaintiffs¿ requests for admission so such requests will not be deemed admitted. Defendant¿s request for sanctions is denied. Plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions is granted in part and denied in part. Defendant is sanctioned in the amount of $1330. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CANDICE CLARK, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR IN et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Plaintiffs Further Responses To Discovery, Production Of Verifications, And For The Imposition Of Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, March 13, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Eaton Corporation¿s motion to compel plaintiffs¿ further responses to special interrogatories is granted as to interrogatories nos. 1, 2, 3, 5 and 6 because plaintiffs¿ responses are inadequate and granted as to interrogatories nos. 4, 10 and 20-31 because plaintiffs do not oppose and have agreed to provide such responses. Defendant¿s motion to compel plaintiffs¿ further responses to form interrogatory no. 17.1 is granted because plaintiffs need to provide a response as to each request for admission and because plaintiffs¿ current response is inadequate. Defendant¿s motion to compel plaintiffs¿ further responses to requests for production nos. 11 and 16 is granted because plaintiffs do not oppose and have agreed to provide such responses. Plaintiffs have 15 days to serve such responses. Defendant¿s request for sanctions is denied for improper notice in violation of C.C.P. 2023.040. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
